description: Cross-view building semantic description dataset. Each sample pairs one satellite nadir image with multiple drone oblique-view images, annotated with a single English paragraph (60-80 words) describing building geometry, appearance, and functional context.

images.temporal_note: The nadir and oblique-view images show consistent details of the building's footprint, orientation, and roof features. However, the oblique views reveal additional context, such as the presence of a yellow-painted section on an adjacent building and more detailed surroundings, including parking lots and nearby roads, which are not fully visible in the nadir view. The overall structure and appearance remain unchanged across the different perspectives.

sentences.raw: The image depicts a single large industrial building with an irregular polygonal footprint, oriented north-south. The structure features a flat, light brown roof with four visible ventilation units and green trim along the edges. The facade appears to be composed of light-colored panels, possibly metal or concrete. Surrounding the building are paved areas, including parking lots filled with vehicles, adjacent roads, and patches of greenery such as trees and grassy fields.

images.temporal_note: The nadir image and oblique views depict the same two buildings consistently. However, the oblique perspectives provide more detail on the surrounding architecture, showing that Building 2 is part of a larger complex with similar red-brick buildings, while the newer structures near Building 1 have light-colored roofs. No inconsistencies in building state or surroundings are evident.

sentences.raw: The nadir image shows two medium-sized buildings in a landscaped area with trees and pathways. Building 1, located in the southeast, has an irregular polygonal footprint with a light-colored roof and appears to be a multi-story structure. Building 2, positioned to the north, is L-shaped or irregular, featuring reddish-brown brick facades and green-roofed sections. Surrounding both buildings are paved roads, parking areas, and dense greenery, suggesting a campus or institutional setting.

images.temporal_note: The nadir and oblique-view images are consistent in showing the completed state of the building. No changes in structure, demolition, or significant alterations to the surroundings are evident across the views. The parking lots, landscaping, and adjacent buildings remain unchanged, indicating a stable scene over time.
